key_insight: The paper shows that physics-augmented neural networks can be embedded as user material models in explicit finite element solvers to reproduce hyperelastic large-strain behavior without custom specialized software.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical machine-learning-based constitutive modeling in impact simulations using existing workflows in Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss.
method: Train hyperelastic PANNs in PyTorch with embedded physical constraints
Transfer the trained architecture and weights into standalone Fortran user material routines
Reduce runtime cost by replacing SoftPlus with SQuarePlus while preserving accuracy
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an impact simulation demonstrating that the generated PANN user material reproduces the nonlinear response of hyperelastic materials under large strains.
watch_item: Performance and accuracy depend on network architecture and activation choice, so overly complex models may become too expensive for explicit solvers.
paper_title: Implementation of Hyperelastic Physics-Augmented Neural Networks in the Explicit Finite Element Codes Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss with Applications to Impact Events
